channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iron-60 in Antarctic Ice Reveals Solar System's Passage Through Interstellar Cloud
item.description: Researchers studying Antarctic ice cores have discovered traces of the rare iron isotope iron-60, which provides insights into Earth's movement through the cosmos. Iron-60 is produced in extreme event...

item.title: SON and Rivers State boost joint efforts to improve compliance with food fortification standards
item.description: The Standards Organisation of Nigeria (SON) and the Rivers State Government don agree to strengthen their partnership to improve compliance with mandatory food fortification standards. This move dey c...

item.title: Police Defend Sh1.9 Billion VIP Security and Expanded Police Budget Amid Financial Concerns
item.description: Nairobi — The National Police Service (NPS) has faced intense scrutiny from MPs over the Sh1.9 billion allocated for security of government buildings and VIP protection in the 2026/2027 budget. Commit...

item.title: Gena Rowlands: Celebrating the Life and Legacy of an Independent Film Icon
item.description: Gena Rowlands (1930–2024) was a monumental figure in American cinema, celebrated for her intense and emotionally raw performances. She is best remembered for her groundbreaking collaborations with dir...

item.title: Political Parties Intensify Efforts to Redraw Congressional Districts
item.description: The ongoing fight over congressional redistricting has escalated into a highly visible political battle. Both Republican and Democratic parties are actively attempting to redraw House district lines t...

item.title: WA mining sector welcomes unchanged tax settings in federal budget
item.description: Western Australia’s resources industry has welcomed the federal budget, praising Treasurer Jim Chalmers for recognising the mining sector’s crucial role in supporting the national economy. Despite ris...

item.title: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Renamed as PMOS to Better Reflect Hormonal and Metabolic Effects
item.description: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome, widely known as PCOS, affects around 170 million women globally and is the leading cause of female infertility. Recent medical research has announced that PCOS will now be r...

item.title: DRC Central Bank Governor Discusses Economic Progress and Financial Reforms
item.description: André Wameso, the governor of the Central Bank of Democratic Republic of Congo, has become a key figure in the country's economic revival. Under his leadership, the Congolese franc experienced a stron...

item.title: House selects 11 prosecutors for Sara Duterte's upcoming impeachment trial
item.description: The House of Representatives has officially chosen an 11-member prosecution team for Vice President Sara Duterte's anticipated impeachment trial before the Senate. This selection followed the approval...

item.title: Ramaphosa to Challenge Constitutional Court Ruling as Regional Conflicts and Investments Make Headlines
item.description: On 12 May 2026, President Cyril Ramaphosa confirmed he will not resign following the Constitutional Court ruling concerning the Section 89 impeachment process linked to the 2020 theft at his Phala Pha...

item.title: New Brunswick municipal elections: Voters select new mayors in Fredericton, Moncton, and re-elect in Saint John
item.description: In Monday’s municipal elections across New Brunswick, voters turned out to choose new leadership in several key cities. Unofficial results indicate that Steve Hicks, a longtime city councillor, won th...

item.title: Treasurer Jim Chalmers Outlines Key Measures in 2026 Federal Budget
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers presented the 2026 Federal Budget, describing it as a response to Australia's fifth economic shock in two decades. Key measures include the Strengthening Australia’s Fuel Resili...

item.title: John Engstead and the Rise of California-Style Fashion Photography in the 1940s
item.description: John Engstead (1909–1983) was a defining figure in Hollywood’s Golden Age photography, known for reshaping the visual language of fashion and celebrity portraiture. Unlike many of his contemporaries w...

item.title: Investment Opportunities in Dodoma: Mining, Agriculture, Tourism and Trade
item.description: Tanzania’s Finance Minister, Ambassador Khamis Mussa Omar, together with Dodoma Regional Commissioner Rosemary Senyamule, has invited both local and foreign investors to explore opportunities in the D...

item.title: WHO Confirms Continued Monitoring After Hantavirus Cases on Cruise Ship
item.description: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus don talk say dem work never finish for controlling hantavirus after some passengers for cruise ship MV Hondius fall sick. Three people don die for this ...

item.title: Senator Zubiri Voices Concern Over Alleged Harassment of Senate Security Staff by NBI
item.description: Senator Juan Miguel Zubiri has publicly condemned reports alleging that staff of the Senate Sergeant-at-Arms were subjected to physical harassment by agents of the National Bureau of Investigation (NB...

item.title: Unable to generate new title - article content not accessible

item.title: Labour leader Starmer evaluates next steps as ministers push for departure timeline
item.description: Labour Party leader Keir Starmer is reportedly considering his next steps as he prepares for a pivotal cabinet meeting. Senior ministers have reportedly urged him to establish a clear timetable for po...

item.title: Patricia Howard Explores Irish Heritage Through Landscapes and Objects
item.description: Patricia Howard's project 'Unknown Ancestors' investigates a largely unrecorded period of Irish history, particularly the Great Famine, a time when photography was scarce. Growing up, Howard had limit...

item.title: Seven Killed, Including Two Police Officers, in Lakki Marwat Blast
item.description: On Tuesday, a tragic blast occurred in Serai Naurang tehsil of Lakki Marwat district, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, claiming the lives of seven people, including two policemen, Adil Jan and Rahatullah. Police r...
